q: If the area of the region bounded by the curves <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo></math>
question: If the area of the region bounded by the curves <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo></math>
solution:
Given,

Equations of curve y2-2y=-x and x+y=0

Now solving above equations we get, x=0,-3

Now plotting the area we get, 

Now from above diagram required area will be

A=∫032y-y2+ydy

⇒A=∫033y-y2dy

⇒A=3y22-y3303

⇒A=272-273

⇒A=276=92 sq. units

So, A=92⇒8A=9×4=36.

subject: Mathematics
q: The parabola $y^2=4 x$ divides the area of the circle $x^2+y^2=5$ in two parts. The area of the smaller part is equal to:
question: The parabola $y^2=4 x$ divides the area of the circle $x^2+y^2=5$ in two parts. The area of the smaller part is equal to:
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & y^2=4 x \\ & x^2+y^2=5\end{aligned}$ $\therefore$ Area of shaded region as shown in the figure will be
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{A}_1=\int_0^1 \sqrt{4 \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}+\int_1^{\sqrt{5}} \sqrt{5-\mathrm{x}^2} \mathrm{dx} \\ & =\frac{4}{3} \cdot\left[\mathrm{x}^{\frac{3}{2}}\right]_0^1+\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}}{2} \sqrt{5-\mathrm{x}^2}+\frac{5}{2} \sin ^{-1} \frac{\mathrm{x}}{\sqrt{5}}\right]_1^{\sqrt{5}} \\ & =\frac{1}{3}+\frac{5 \pi}{4}-\frac{5}{2} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}\right) \\ & \therefore \text { Required Area }=2 \mathrm{~A}_1 \\ & =\frac{2}{3}+\frac{5 \pi}{2}-5 \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}\right) \\ & =\frac{2}{3}+5\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\sin ^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}\right) \\ & =\frac{2}{3}+5 \cos ^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{5}} \\ & =\frac{2}{3}+5 \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}\right)\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: The area (in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>sq</mi><mo>.</mo><mi>units</mi></math>) of the region <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>{</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>y</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>≥</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>≤</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>x</mi><mi> </mi><mo>≥</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>y</mi><mi> </mi><mo>≥</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>}</mo><mi> </mi></math> is
question: The area (in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>sq</mi><mo>.</mo><mi>units</mi></math>) of the region <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>{</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>y</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>≥</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>≤</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>x</mi><mi> </mi><mo>≥</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>y</mi><mi> </mi><mo>≥</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>}</mo><mi> </mi></math> is
solution:
y2≥2x  (Area outside the parabola)

x2+y2≤4x (Area inside the circle)


Finding point of intersection of the curves, we get, 

x2+y2=4x and y2=2x

⇒x2+2x=4x

⇒x2=2x

⇒x=0, x=2

If x=0,then y=0 and if x=2, then y=±2.

Coordinates of A0, 0, B2, 2

As x ≥0 ,y ≥0 only area above x-axis would be considered.

Hence, area

=∫024x-x2 dx- 2 ∫02x dx 

=∫024-x-22 dx- 2 ∫02x dx

=x-22 4x-x2+42 .sin-1x-22- 2 x323202

=0-2sin-1-1- 2 .2322=π-83  sq.units

question: The area bounded by the curves y=x-1+x-2 and y=3 is equal to

⇒fx=3-2x:  x<11;         1≤x<22x-3;   x≥2

Let us draw diagram of y=fx & y=3 we get,

Required area is the area of trapezium which is

Area =121+3×2

=4 sq. units

solution:
The area of the region OCD is
=∫014-x2+3xdx=4x-x33-3x2201=4-13-32=136
So, the area of the given region OABC is
= Area of OABD - Area of OCD
=3×4-136
=596

q: The area enclosed by the curves <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mfenced><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mi>y</mi></mfrac></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mn>2</mn></math>, above the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> is
question: The area enclosed by the curves <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mfenced><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mi>y</mi></mfrac></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mn>2</mn></math>, above the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> is
solution:
The area enclosed by the curves y=logex+e2, x=loge2y and x=loge2, above the line y=1 is

Now plotting the diagram of given functions we get,

According to JEE Mains, we have to calculate the required region A2 which is shaded in crossed lines and comes out to be

A2=∫12ln2y-ey+e2dy=1+e-ln2

But according to the question the required region A1 comes out to be shaded in parallel lines, which can be obtained as

=x+e2lnx+e2-x+2e-x0ln2

Not given in any option.

subject: Mathematics
q: The area (in square units) of the region enclosed by the ellipse $x^2+3 y^2=18$ in the first quadrant below the line $y=x$ is
question: The area (in square units) of the region enclosed by the ellipse $x^2+3 y^2=18$ in the first quadrant below the line $y=x$ is
solution:
$\frac{x^2}{18}+\frac{y^2}{6}=1$

$\begin{aligned} & \frac{x^2}{18}+\frac{3 x^2}{18}=1 \Rightarrow 4 x^2=18 \Rightarrow x^2=\frac{9}{2} \\ & \int_{\frac{3}{\sqrt{2}}}^{\sqrt[3]{2}} \frac{\sqrt{18-x^2}}{\sqrt{3}} d x \\ & =\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\left(\frac{x \sqrt{18-x^2}}{2}+\frac{18}{2} \sin ^{-1} \frac{x}{3 \sqrt{2}}\right)_{\frac{3}{\sqrt{2}}}^{3 \sqrt{2}} \\ & =\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\left(9 \times \frac{\pi}{2}-\frac{3}{2 \sqrt{2}} \times \frac{3 \sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{2}}-9 \times \frac{\pi}{6}\right) \end{aligned}$
Required Area $\begin{aligned} & =\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{9}{2}+\left(\frac{18 \pi}{6}-\frac{9 \sqrt{3}}{4}\right) \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \\ & =\sqrt{3} \pi \end{aligned}$
